channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Potholes some with whit lines but not all, 9th March
item.description:
B4364 Old Gloucester Road Springbank

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Old Gloucester Road, Springbank, Cheltenham

item.title: Blocked drain, 9th March
item.description:
Corner of parking area outside 90 Ashlands Road. Already reported twice, and both times closed within a week without investigation.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 78-88, Ashlands Road, Kingsditch, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
03/03/26 email rcvd (attached) reporting leftover cone
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles problem, 9th March
item.description:
03/03/26 email rcvd (attached )reporting leftover TM signs after utility works
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: Ongoing uneven road, 9th March
item.description:
I've reported this before, and over the years there has been various works done. Currently the pothole around the sewage access is not bad, but my house (and probably others) shakes constantly with the traffic. Root cause is more the inappropriate use of the road, we get lorries and industrial traffic on this road and it's treated like an A road. There are no traffic calming measures and the road is not engineered to handle that level of traffic. How do we escalate these kinds of design problems, and when the house develops cracks, how do we seek recompense for the remedial works?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Carlton Street, Fairview, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 9th March
item.description:
03/03/26 email rcvd (attached) reporting group of potholes at location
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple flags cabled tied to lampposts, 9th March
item.description:
Multiple flags have been cable tied to lampposts along Stockwell lane and Station road. They appear to be unauthorised items attached to street furniture. They are attached with cabke tied and run along multiple lampposts.They look like illegal signage and are offensive and distracting to drivers Please could highways inspect and remove them

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stockwell Lane, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: White plastic wrapping and pipe work (red and black) been left behind after clear up., 9th March
item.description:
On grass verge by path. Been there for at least a week. All excess pipes were collected last week but didn’t pick up this items

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A40, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor

item.title: Us flags put up along street, 9th March
item.description:
Very offensive to all effected by their warmongering

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stockwell Lane, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass, 9th March
item.description:
Broken glass on foot path in underpass

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A40, Elmbridge, Gloucester

item.title: Button push on crossing times out and needs repressing to activate cross, 9th March
item.description:
Pedestrian crossing at Pirton corner
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cheltenham Road East, Churchdown, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Whole road of unsafe potholes, 9th March
item.description:
The entirety of the road outside the ladies college health and fitness centre. Children have tripped and hurt themselves because of the state of the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Malvern Road, Lansdown, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Collapsed trench across road, 8th March
item.description:
There is a collapsed trench just after the merge after the traffic lights heading away from GCHQ. Causing damage to suspensions
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Princess Elizabeth Way, Benhall, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big, quite deep pothole very close to right turn into The Reddings., 8th March
item.description:
Big, quite deep pothole very close to right turn into The Reddings coming from Churchdown direction. It's avoidable in daylight but could cause serious damage if hit. Difficult to miss in the dark. Very dangerous as close to frequently used turning. Has appeared in the last few days I think.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Badgeworth Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive longitudinal potholes, 8th March
item.description:
On the road outside the Greyhound Pub and at the chicane near the playground. It is necessary to take evasive action to avoid these potholes and involves driving on the wrong side of the road, very dangerous situation

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greyhound Gardens, Elmbridge, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waymarker removed from fence, path obscured, 8th March
item.description:
This issue was reported in August 2025 and there has been no action. The waymarker for this path has been removed, a sign directed into a different PRoW and a series of field gates now obscures the path. There has been no application to change, remove or divert this PRoW
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockhampton Lane, Kingsditch, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road surface material dumped in verge, 8th March
item.description:
Following recent repairs to some of the (many) potholes down this road, road surface material has been strewn in the verge by the contractor rather than being removed. This is disappointing and looks a mess, and is presumably not what is meant to happen to waste.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockhampton Lane, Kingsditch, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is a sewage leak into the surface water drainage swale that runs beside the Coopers Edge Play Area. It is coming from the culvert than runs in from Glebe Hardens/ Guan Road. Upstream of the culvert the water is clear. The ducks that were there have gone. There is darks brown sludge and surface scum. Location on what three words around ///slap.anyone.splice but it continues towards the reed bed beside Lobleys Drive. It appeared 3 days ago and I have also reported it to the EA. Regards Claire Stewart, 8th March
item.description:
There is a sewage leak into the surface water drainage swale that runs beside the Coopers Edge Play Area. It is coming from the culvert than runs in from Glebe Hardens/ Guan Road. Upstream of the culvert the water is clear. The ducks that were there have gone. There is darks brown sludge and surface scum. Location on what three words around ///slap.anyone.splice but it continues towards the reed bed beside Lobleys Drive. It appeared 3 days ago and I have also reported it to the EA. Regards Claire Stewart

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carter Close, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Failed surface, 8th March
item.description:
The road surface has failed and is disintegrating entirely in this location forming potholes

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hawker Road, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road subsiding and numerous potholes, 8th March
item.description:
The road surface is subsiding and disintegrating right on a 90 degree bend in the road such that it is practically impossible to keep on the correct side of the road on a blind bend

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockhampton Lane, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet
